codeReview.summary: Implementation integrates loop guard into standalone-server.ts to prevent infinite tool calling loops. The implementation: (1) Imports createToolLoopGuard and parseToolLoopMaxRepeat from provider/tool-loop-guard, (2) Creates a loop guard instance using messages array to track conversation history, (3) Evaluates each tool call with guard before forwarding to client, (4) If triggered, emits error chunk with descriptive message and terminates SSE stream.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The TOOL_LOOP_MAX_REPEAT environment variable is used to configure the loop guard but is not documented in .factory/services.yaml. However, this is non-blocking since the env var has a default value (2) and is purely optional.
sharedStateObservations.implication: Users who want to adjust the loop threshold need to know about this env var. Could be documented in services.yaml if this is a common configuration need.

summary: Implementation correctly handles tool call loop integration. Loop guard is properly initialized with messages history and evaluates each tool call before forwarding. When triggered, an error is emitted and stream terminates. Tool results are correctly forwarded in subsequent requests via buildPrompt formatting. All assertions (VAL-TOOL-001, VAL-TOOL-002, VAL-TOOL-003) are fulfilled. Verification commands (typecheck, build, tests) all passed successfully. No blocking issues found.
